title: neea subcoccinea
intro: Neea subcoccinea, also known as the "red flower neea," is a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The health benefits of Neea subcoccinea include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote skin healing, making it a valuable remedy for various health conditions. Neea subcoccinea can be prepared as an herbal remedy through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, from minor skin irritations to more serious infections.
